notes: Supplemental scan May 21, 2026 — full sweep using all 31 sources including 22 newly added Maryland county procurement portals. No new opportunities cleared the scoring threshold. Candidates reviewed and rejected: Charles County Youth Mentoring RFP 26-020 (posted Nov 28, 2025; deadline unconfirmed but almost certainly expired given 30-60 day typical timeline, likely Jan 2026); Allegany County Tourism Strategic Planning RFP (deadline was Nov 15, 2024, plan completed Aug 2025 — confirmed closed); Montgomery County HHS open solicitations are standing vendor pools with no fixed deadline, not competitive RFPs; Carroll County consulting RFPs had Feb 2026 deadlines (past); Calvert County economic development strategic plan was already awarded to Sage Policy Group; St. Mary's County nonprofit funding program deadline was Jan 12, 2026 (past) and requires 501c3 direct-service providers; Cecil County CAD evaluation consultant out-of-sector; Washington and Wicomico county portals use OpenGov/CivicEngage JavaScript rendering with no qualifying solicitations surfaced via web search; Howard County PlanetBids portal (JavaScript-rendered) shows open solicitations with May-June 2026 deadlines but no consulting/advisory categories confirmed. Pipeline holds steady at 8 active opportunities. URGENT: rfp-002 (Enterprise Community Partners Program Evaluation) due May 28 (7 days). rfp-004 (NFWF Chesapeake Bay INSR) due May 31 (10 days). rfp-008 (MSDE Early Childhood Mental Health) due June 1 (11 days).

rfps.title: From Insight to Action: Health Equity Research That Meets This Moment
rfps.summary: Robert Wood Johnson Foundation seeks proposals for community-rooted, action-oriented health equity research projects. The work involves equitable community partnerships, evaluation, and systems-change recommendations tied to RWJF's seven prioritized systems. Award size is up to $500,000 per project with full proposals due July 27, 2026.

rfps.title: General Support for Program Evaluation and Related Services
rfps.summary: Enterprise Community Partners is selecting evaluation partners to conduct formative, process, and summative evaluations across its housing and community development portfolio. Scope also includes evaluation-related training, technical assistance, and capacity support for grantees and internal staff. Proposals are due May 28, 2026.

rfps.title: FY2026 Local Rehabilitation Agency (LRA) Request for Applications
rfps.issuer: Maryland Department of Housing and Community Development
rfps.summary: Maryland DHCD is accepting applications from Local Rehabilitation Agencies to administer community development and housing rehabilitation programs across the state. The work involves program design, technical assistance to local jurisdictions, and ongoing program management. Applications are due June 16, 2026 with projects beginning on or after July 1, 2026.

rfps.title: Chesapeake Bay Innovative Nutrient and Sediment Reduction (INSR) Grants 2026
rfps.summary: NFWF and EPA are funding watershed restoration and water quality projects across the Chesapeake Bay region through the Stewardship Fund. The opportunity supports planning, technical assistance, and on-the-ground restoration work that advances local capacity for nutrient and sediment reduction. Full proposals are due May 31, 2026.

rfps.title: FY2026 Consolidated Request for Proposals (Housing and Community Development)
rfps.issuer: DC Department of Housing and Community Development
rfps.summary: DC DHCD is soliciting proposals for community development, housing rehabilitation, and neighborhood revitalization activities across the District. Scope includes program design, technical assistance to community partners, and strategic planning for neighborhood-level investments. Submissions are due June 30, 2026.

rfps.title: Virginia Board for People with Disabilities 2026 Competitive RFP
rfps.issuer: Virginia Board for People with Disabilities
rfps.source: Virginia Board for People with Disabilities
rfps.summary: The Virginia Board for People with Disabilities is funding projects that build community capacity and advance inclusion for Virginians with developmental disabilities. Total funding of up to $200,000 is available across one or more awards. The work supports program design, evaluation, and capacity building with community partners.

rfps.title: FY2027 Rural Maryland Council Grant Cycle (MAERDAF and RMPIF)
rfps.source: Maryland Governor's Office for Children Newsletter, May 12, 2026
rfps.summary: The Rural Maryland Council opened its FY2027 grant cycle, with a total of about $6 million across two programs. MAERDAF supports rural-serving nonprofits and community colleges providing technical assistance and capacity building for agriculture, small business, and rural economic development. RMPIF funds local and regional public and nonprofit organizations advancing rural economic and community development. Phase 1 Letters of Intent are due May 15, 2026 and Phase 2 full applications are due July 10, 2026.

rfps.title: FY2027 Infant and Early Childhood Mental Health Support Services Program
rfps.issuer: Maryland State Department of Education, Division of Early Childhood
rfps.source: Maryland Governor's Office for Children Newsletter, May 12, 2026
rfps.summary: MSDE is accepting applications for grants that promote positive mental and behavioral health practices for children from birth through age five. The program builds the capacity of early care and education providers and family members through training, consultation, and family support services. Eligible applicants include community-based organizations and local education agencies. Applications are due June 1, 2026.
